claim: The novelty reserve should track the recent RATE of newcomers. A reader whose reserve amplitude is the γ-decayed rate of first-appearances assigns a newcomer-into-a-settled-context far MORE surprise than the same newcomer-into-a-churning-context; a reader with a constant reserve, blind to novelty history, assigns the SAME surprise to both because the two contexts are matched on total mass.
mechanism: noveltyAmplitude (src/core/surprise.js) — Σ γ^(at-1-j) over first-appearances j<at — fed as the `novelty` amplitude through the UNCHANGED Born step reserve = novelty/(Σmass+novelty). Context enters at the amplitude; the law is untouched. The same recipe feeds the text figure-field reader and the n-gram sequence reader.

A.context: six distinct units — every step a newcomer, a high novelty rate

B.context: one unit repeated — pure confirmation after step 0, novelty rate → 0

C.context: one unit repeated — the standing figure carries the MAXIMAL prior mass (loudest surface)
C.probe: a confirmation of that loud figure (zero novelty)

flag_off.text.surprisalBits: A == B (the constant reserve is blind to the novelty rate: matched mass → identical)
flag_off.note: this identity IS the gap the fix closes; it pins the byte-identical default

flag_on.text.surprisalBits: B > A by a clear margin (a newcomer shocks a settled reader, barely moves a churning one)
flag_on.seq.pNovel: on raises A's reserve above its flag-off value (stay open) and lowers B's below its flag-off value (settle) — the two-way move a constant cannot make

control.predicted: text.surprisalBits(C) is LOW and far below B under flag on: the quiet newcomer (B's probe) dominates the loud confirmation (C's probe). A method scoring by surface presence/mass would rank C's loud standing figure above B's unseen newcomer — backwards. Catching that is the control.
control.trivial_explanation_it_kills: surprise ∝ total mass / step count / the probe's surface presence
